what_it_does: A runnable acceptance-test script that verifies the model capability registry (cap.MODEL_CAPABILITIES) and its integration with gpu.py, registry loading, role binding, placement policy, cloud decoupling, and fail-loud residency semantics.
observations: It is a standalone script with a hand-rolled check() helper and global pass/fail counters.
It inserts ops/cli into sys.path so it can import registry, gpu, and capabilities.
It hard-codes a resident model id (cyankiwi/Qwen3.5-4B-AWQ-4bit) and a cloud model id (deepseek-v4-pro:cloud).
It asserts exact numeric constants: context_ceiling 262144, vram_budget_mb 8024, max_num_seqs 16, swarm KV 135574.
Every capability field is expected to be a {value, source} dict with a valid provenance source.
It tests that gpu.budget_vram is reused as the single authority for VRAM rather than duplicated.
It checks role_can_bind, suitable_models, provides_for, placement_for, and require_resident semantics.
Live probing is read-only and skipped (not failed) when the resident service at :8000 is down.
standouts: The test doubles as a specification document, tagging each section with requirement IDs C8.1-C8.4 and C9.4.
It explicitly validates the JOIN between capabilities and gpu.py, including exact reuse of gpu.budget_vram.
It encodes fail-loud residency behavior: require_resident returns OFFER_LOAD with a load_command instead of auto-loading.
It distinguishes served vs declared provenance and tests that live probes win over declared values.
conventions: Follows acceptance-test naming with requirement IDs in comments and section headers.
Uses bare imports and sys.path manipulation to reach ops/cli modules.
Uses global mutable counters for pass/fail tally.
Breaks typical unit-test isolation by probing a live local service and depending on a specific model being resident.
Uses plain print-based reporting rather than a unittest/pytest harness.
concerns: Hard-coded model ids and exact numeric constants make the test brittle to configuration or measurement drift.
Environment-dependent live service checks can cause different outcomes on different machines.
Global mutable state is not thread-safe and complicates parallel test execution.
sys.path manipulation creates a fragile coupling to repository layout.
Some assertions mix complex logic with detail formatting, reducing readability.
notes: A requirement-tagged acceptance runner that treats the model capability registry and its gpu.py JOIN as executable design contracts.
questions: Where are cap.MODEL_CAPABILITIES, gpu.budget_vram, and registry.load defined and how are they populated?
What is the C0.5 measurement source and how are the numeric constants kept in sync with it?
How is the live probe implemented and what endpoint or data does it read on :8000?
What is the exact schema of the registry object and the service config used by gpu.budget_vram?
How are provenance values validated beyond membership in a predefined set?
purpose_vs_actual: Matches: the docstring states it proves C8.1-C8.4 and C9.4 by use, and main() runs checks organized under exactly those requirement IDs.
apparent_themes: Single source of truth for VRAM through gpu.py
Fail-loud explicit behavior instead of silent degradation
Live-proven capabilities with served vs declared provenance
intention_signals: Encode the model-capability design contract as a runnable acceptance suite so that regressions in registry data, the gpu.py JOIN, role binding, placement policy, or residency semantics are caught immediately.
novelty: It treats an acceptance test as a living design document tied to requirement IDs and external measurement constants, and it explicitly asserts that VRAM is joined from gpu.py rather than duplicated.
contribution: A runnable, requirement-tagged acceptance suite that validates the model capability registry's data shape, provenance rules, JOIN with gpu.py, role binding, placement policy, and fail-loud residency semantics.
summary_for_embedding: An acceptance test script that verifies the model capability registry's fields, provenance, gpu.py JOIN, role binding, placement policy, and fail-loud residency behavior for resident and cloud models.
intention_for_embedding: To prove by use that the model capability registry and its surrounding services satisfy the C8.1-C8.4 and C9.4 design contracts.

symbols.does: Records a pass/fail result for a named assertion and prints the outcome.
symbols.description: The check function is the test harness primitive: it takes a name, a boolean condition, and an optional detail string, increments the global pass or fail counter, and prints a PASS or FAIL line. Every assertion in the script flows through this function.

symbols.does: Loads the registry and runs the full acceptance checklist for model capabilities.
symbols.description: The main function loads the registry, then walks through requirement-tagged sections (C9.4 hygiene, C8.1 served fields, C8.2 gpu.py JOIN and role queries, C8.3 cloud/placement, C8.4 residency, and a live probe), calling check() for each assertion. It finishes by printing the total passed/failed count and exits with status 1 if any check failed.
